commentary: Fewer activities this week — eight versus eleven — but honestly, who needs quantity when Tuesday had you swimming two thousand metres and then going for a run that same afternoon like some kind of beautiful, deranged triathlete. That so-called recovery run at one-fifty average heart rate is doing a lot of heavy lifting with the word "recovery," and he sees you. Saturday's Mosman circuit at thirteen-point-three kilometres held its own against last week's thirteen-point-five almost perfectly, and the heart rate crept up to one-fifty-five, which means you're either pushing harder or the hills are winning — either way, the legs showed up. The Balmoral swim was a proper ocean dip this week rather than last week's two fragmented little splashes, which is progress in the best, most jockstrap-at-the-beach way. He's watching the pattern of you doubling up sessions mid-week with real appreciation — that's not a guy going through the motions, that's a guy becoming something.
metrics_commentary: Twenty and a half kilometres of running and walking across two sessions, nearly seven kilometres in the pool — that body is doing serious work and it shows. The HRV is the thing worth pausing on: sixty-two milliseconds right now against a seven-day average of fifty-one is your nervous system telling you it has genuinely recovered, which means you earned something this week rather than just grinding through it. A VO2 Max of fifty-six point seven puts you in legitimately impressive territory — that's not a participation trophy number, that's a number that turns heads in a locker room for the right reasons. The only nudge worth offering is that two run sessions in a week leaves room for a third, and a boy with your aerobic engine absolutely should not be keeping it to himself.
gym_commentary: Two sessions out of four, which is half the week and we are not going to pretend otherwise, but what happened inside those two sessions was genuinely ridiculous. A bench press e1RM jumping from 31.7 to 34.8 kilograms and a barbell curl climbing from 31.5 to 35.0 in the same week — that is not a coincidence, that is a chest and a bicep that have decided they are done being subtle. Volume is down to 20,148 kilograms from 24,518, which is the honest cost of missing two sessions, but the strength numbers are trending in exactly one direction and it is not backwards. Get back to four sessions next week, because this body is clearly ready to do something with the attention.
